step_by_step_reasoning: We identify two main workflows: 1) DevOps Management where developers access an S3-hosted React app, which calls API Gateway to trigger Lambda functions managing EC2 clusters and AWS Organizations (governed by Control Tower). 2) Appway User cluster where end-users (bank agents) authenticate via Cognito, access application nodes on EC2 via an ALB, with shared storage on EFS and connection to on-premises core banking systems via Site-to-Site VPN.

graph.name: Appway SaaS Multi-Tenant Architecture
graph.notes: Ensures strong customer isolation using AWS Organizations for single-tenant accounts per client. Appway operates as a multi-node Tomcat cluster in ASG using Hazelcast for synchronization and EFS for shared cluster storage.

nodes.name: DevOps Engineers
nodes.notes: Appway's cloud automation team managing client accounts.

nodes.name: Management API Gateway
nodes.notes: Receives calls from the management frontend.

nodes.name: Account Provisioner & Deployment
nodes.notes: Executes DevOps tasks like creating accounts and software updates.

nodes.name: AWS Organizations
nodes.notes: Manages dedicated AWS accounts per customer.

nodes.name: AWS Control Tower
nodes.notes: Enforces security policies and guardrails consistently.

nodes.name: Bank Staff / End Users
nodes.notes: Users onboarding bank clients every 30 seconds.

nodes.name: Amazon Cognito
nodes.notes: Handles user auth, federated with AD/Okta if needed.

nodes.name: Application Load Balancer
nodes.notes: Handles request routing and SSL offloading.

nodes.name: Appway Cluster Instances
nodes.notes: Runs Java-based web application with Tomcat and Hazelcast.

nodes.name: Amazon EFS
nodes.notes: Central cluster storage supporting growing data needs.

nodes.name: Site-to-Site VPN
nodes.notes: Provides dedicated secure channel to customer network.

nodes.name: On-Premises Bank Systems
nodes.notes: External systems such as core banking, AD/Okta, or document archives.

edges.notes: Developer loads management console web app.
Sets up and manages dedicated tenant accounts.
Applies software updates and configuration changes.
Logs in and requests authentication token.
Routes traffic to active EC2 instances.
Reads and writes to shared cluster file system.
Connects to bank's core system / IdP.
Passes data back to Appway application.
Sends application response back to load balancer.
Delivers dynamic content back to the end-user.
